entries.title: As freed ComEd defendants face new trial, jurors say they already considered ‘every aspect’ of the case
entries.summary: <p>Before they <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/comed-bribery-trial/2026/04/14/madigan-bribery-comed-conspiracy-bribery-chicago-crime-pramaggiore-mcclain" target="_blank" >freed two former ComEd officials</a> who went to prison over an alleged corruption scheme<b> </b>involving Illinois’ most powerful politician, federal appeals court judges asked a lot of questions about the case — but mostly <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/2023/11/3/23919713/corruption-jurors-ed-burke-trial" target="_blank" >about the jury</a> that found the pair guilty.</p><p>For example, how could the judges be sure the jury didn’t focus solely on bribery claims that have since been undermined by the U.S. Supreme Court?</p><p>The Chicago Sun-Times asked three of the 12 jurors Wednesday. They said they carefully considered every claim leveled against former ComEd CEO Anne<b> </b>Pramaggiore and longtime ComEd lobbyist Michael McClain — including those that survived a <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/chicago-corruption-trials/2024/06/26/supreme-court-snyder-ruling-madigan-corruption" target="_blank" >2024 Supreme Court ruling</a>.</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/comed-bribery-trial/2026/04/14/madigan-bribery-comed-conspiracy-bribery-chicago-crime-pramaggiore-mcclain" >Ex-ComEd CEO, Madigan ally to be freed from prison as appeals court calls for new trial in conspiracy case</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p>“We concluded they all intertwined and related to each other,” Robert<b> </b>Garnes, one of the jurors in the 2023 ComEd conspiracy trial, told the Sun-Times.</p><p>The ComEd trial <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/2023/5/2/23697452/jurors-reach-verdict-in-comed-bribery-trial" target="_blank" >ended with the convictions</a> of McClain, Pramaggiore<b> </b>and two others of a plot to bribe<b> </b>ex-Illinois House Speaker <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/madigan-trial-news" target="_blank" >Michael J. Madigan</a>. The high court’s later decision to limit a key bribery law undermined the verdict and led to the dismissal of the bribery counts in 2025. But convictions for conspiracy and falsifying books and records remained.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-floatRight> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-690000" name="image-690000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/f11e951/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fb9%2F77%2Fc046af4e517d9f5969693b2e1aab%2Fmerlin-116723840-1.jpg data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/7fe72ef/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fb9%2F77%2Fc046af4e517d9f5969693b2e1aab%2Fmerlin-116723840-1.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="merlin-116723840-1.jpg" srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/7fe72ef/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fb9%2F77%2Fc046af4e517d9f5969693b2e1aab%2Fmerlin-116723840-1.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/128a8ec/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fb9%2F77%2Fc046af4e517d9f5969693b2e1aab%2Fmerlin-116723840-1.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/7fe72ef/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fb9%2F77%2Fc046af4e517d9f5969693b2e1aab%2Fmerlin-116723840-1.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>ComEd juror Robert Garnes poses for a portrait in Westmont, Illinois Wednesday, October 18, 2023. | Anthony Vazquez/Sun-Times</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Anthony Vazquez/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>Then the 7th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals took things further Tuesday. It promised to order a new trial and told the feds to release Pramaggiore and McClain from their two-year prison sentences “forthwith.”<b> </b>The news came four hours after a three-judge panel heard arguments in the case.</p><p>The Federal Bureau of Prisons confirmed the release of McClain and Pramaggiore late Wednesday afternoon.</p><p>The appeals court’s order had no direct impact on <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/madigan-trial-news/mike-madigan-guilty-verdict" target="_blank" >Madigan’s separate conviction</a>, which came in February 2025. The 2023 trial of McClain and Pramaggiore also ended with the convictions of longtime ComEd lobbyist John Hooker and former City Club President Jay Doherty, but they did not appeal and have served their sentences.</p><p>During Tuesday’s arguments in the ComEd case, Judges<b> </b>Thomas<b> </b>Kirsch<b> </b>and<b> </b>Joshua<b> </b>Kolar<b> </b>seemed particularly concerned with the remaining conspiracy conviction against McClain and Pramaggiore.</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/2023/11/3/23919713/corruption-jurors-ed-burke-trial" >‘Facts and common sense’: Chicago’s corruption jurors reflect on past trials as Ed Burke’s case nears</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p>It featured multiple “objects”<b> </b>for the jury to consider, including bribery and falsifying records.</p><p>Given the feds’ emphasis on bribery during the trial, as well as the Supreme Court’s ruling, the judges wanted assurance that jurors reached a verdict that included false records as an object of the conspiracy, rather than an invalid bribery theory.</p><p>“Here’s the problem,” Kirsch told a prosecutor. “When you charge the case and try the case as a bribery case, what’s to say the jury just didn’t consider the illegal bribery object in the conspiracy and stop right there?”</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-floatLeft> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-7f0000" name="image-7f0000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/ee94375/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5666x3180+0+299/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F71%2Fae%2Fc3d87747db10e75b7616d21626b2%2Fsarah-goldenberg-comed-juror.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/c659b3a/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5666x3180+0+299/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F71%2Fae%2Fc3d87747db10e75b7616d21626b2%2Fsarah-goldenberg-comed-juror.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/3ee5463/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5666x3180+0+299/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F71%2Fae%2Fc3d87747db10e75b7616d21626b2%2Fsarah-goldenberg-comed-juror.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="sarah-goldenberg-comed-juror.jpg" srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/3ee5463/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5666x3180+0+299/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F71%2Fae%2Fc3d87747db10e75b7616d21626b2%2Fsarah-goldenberg-comed-juror.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/fb5d9a5/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5666x3180+0+299/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F71%2Fae%2Fc3d87747db10e75b7616d21626b2%2Fsarah-goldenberg-comed-juror.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/3ee5463/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5666x3180+0+299/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F71%2Fae%2Fc3d87747db10e75b7616d21626b2%2Fsarah-goldenberg-comed-juror.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Marc Monaghan / WBEZ</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>Sarah Goldenberg,<b> </b>the foreperson of the ComEd jury, said the conspiracy count “was actually one of the last ones we looked at,” even though it appeared as Count One<b> </b>in the indictment.</p><p>“That was the big behemoth of it all,” Goldenberg said. “We actually did really parse it out and look at each individual charge and looked at each object of it.”</p><p>Another juror, Amanda<b> </b>Schnitker Sayers, agreed with Goldenberg’s recollection. She said, “we almost worked it backwards” during the 27-hour deliberations.</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/comed-bribery-trial/2026/04/14/madigan-bribery-comed-conspiracy-bribery-chicago-crime-pramaggiore-mcclain" >Ex-ComEd CEO, Madigan ally to be freed from prison as appeals court calls for new trial in conspiracy case</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p>“For each individual count we took them all separate and we went through and we found the evidence for each one,” Schnitker Sayers said.</p><p>Garnes said it all “went hand in hand.”</p><p>“We took in every aspect of the trial that was presented before us,” he said.</p><p>The jurors’ comments are unlikely to influence what happens next in the case. But they provide insight that’s otherwise unavailable in the courtroom. The jurors weren’t required to mark the objects of the conspiracy on the verdict form, and U.S. District Judge Harry<b> </b>Leinenweber<b> </b>declined<b> </b>a defense request to question the jury<b> </b>in May 2023.</p><p>All three of the jurors said they would have been open to an inquiry that could have resolved the questions. Schnitker Sayers raised a concern about future efforts to invade the privacy of the jury room, though.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-floatRight> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/5e729db/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fd4%2Fed%2F4918607c049467a09421b8329634%2Fmerlin-116555458.jpg" class="Figure-content"><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Anthony Vazquez / Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>By the time deliberations came around, she said they all felt free to question each other. Garnes even said they “fought and hollered and screamed and threw things” but<b> </b>“finally came to an agreement.”</p><p>“And we loved each other for it,” he added.</p><p>As to whether the feds should move forward with a new trial, Schnitker Sayers had mixed feelings. She said the four defendants were “good people who did illegal things,”<b> </b>and not a danger to society.</p><p>But, she asked, “are we allowed now to act in the way that they acted?”</p><p>Garnes said a new trial suggests the first one ended in a wrongful conviction.</p><p>“I think it should be over,” he said.</p><p>And Goldenberg said it would be a waste of tax dollars.</p><p>“I believe they will be found guilty again,” she said, “because that’s what we did.”</p>
entries.author: Jon Seidel

entries.title: Sky's Skylar Diggins wanted to be closer to home, but she’s not just coasting into retirement
entries.summary: <p>To be a <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/chicago-sky" target="_blank" >Sky</a> fan right now is to be bursting with questions. Ones like: What is Skylar Diggins doing in Chicago?</p><p>It surprised many to see the seven-time All-Star choose a team that has won less than 30% of its games over the last two seasons, and an organization not exactly known for flawless execution since its 2021 championship.</p><p>So how did she end up here?</p><p>As the new collective-bargaining agreement reshaped the league this offseason, Diggins took a moment to reflect.</p><p>She thought about where she was in her career — 35 years old, two kids, entering her 12th season — and decided she wanted a change. She's played on the West Coast her whole career, but she grew up in South Bend, 90 miles outside of Chicago.</p><p>She wanted to be closer to home.</p><p>And Chicago successfully sold her on its vision: a win-now roster that was tougher, more athletic, more competitive than last year.</p><p>For head coach Tyler Marsh, Diggins' arrival is proof the organization is moving in the right direction.</p><p>"Skylar has been around this league a long time to know the fake from the real," Marsh said. "And there's a reason she wanted to be here. Being close to home helps, but you also gotta be able to trust the people you're going to compete with. There's some discernment there that she's able to recognize."</p><p>The Sky did execute well in free agency, landing <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/chicago-sky/2026/04/14/azura-stevens-returns-to-chicago-where-the-fit-and-the-money-made-sense" >stretch big Azurá Stevens</a> and two-way wing DiJonai Carrington, and adding young talent in Rickea Jackson and Jacy Sheldon.</p><p>But executing the "win-now" part won't be easy. Most of the league's top teams retained their cores. And even with all the added talent, Diggins is still the Sky's only All-Star.</p><p>Which means the Sky better hope she didn't come back to the Midwest just to decompress before retiring.</p><p>Lucky for them, she shot down the idea before anyone thought to raise it.</p><p>"I'm not just coming here saying this is the end or whatever for me," she said at her introductory news conference. "I don't think it'll be a diminishing return. I'm committed to the work. … I want to get better. That's been my mantra since I was a kid — 'get better.' That's the mindset I have here for this organization, for myself, and that's the standard I hold myself to."</p><p>Even for players with the highest standards, some decline in their game is inevitable. Over the last two years, Diggins' scoring average has settled around 15, down from the 17-20 range of her prime. But pair that with six assists and you're still looking at one of the best point guards in the league.</p><p>So, Sky fans: is this great-player-comes-home-at-the-tail-end-of-her-career storyline giving anyone a Candace Parker flashback? Will this homecoming end in a championship, too?</p><p>One can hope. But there are a few big differences between then and now. As good as Diggins is, she's not quite Parker. And Parker came home to a roster that had been together for years. Diggins is walking into a complete overhaul.</p><p>She'll need a little magic to bring them together ahead of schedule.</p><p></p><bsp-list-loadmore class="PageListH" data-module entries.author: Alissa Hirsh

entries.title: After quick struggles for Shane Smith, White Sox aim to 'slow down his world' at Triple-A
entries.summary: <p>After an awful opening week, the <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox" target="_blank" ><u>White Sox</u></a>’ starting rotation has been a bright spot with an MLB-best 1.73 ERA in 11 games from April 3-14.</p><p>If you were told in spring training that Sox starters would go on a heater like that, you probably would’ve expected Opening Day hurler <b>Shane Smith </b>to be on top of his game — not down on the farm trying to rediscover his All-Star form from last season.</p><p>For Smith to run it back to the big leagues, he’s got to stop running away from the pitch that made him a Rule 5 success story, general manager <b>Chris Getz</b> said a week after the surprising demotion.</p><p>“He was running away from using his four-seam fastball. Just the overall quality of that pitch, along with his curveball, just wasn't up to his standard,” Getz said of the righty, who covered only 8⅓ innings in his three Sox starts, surrendering 12 runs before <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/sports/2026/04/08/white-sox-option-opening-day-starter-shane-smith-to-triple-a-charlotte" target="_blank" ><u>getting shipped to Triple-A Charlotte</u></a>. “We felt like it was best to kind of slow down his world a little bit, get him in an environment that is not so focused on getting so deep into games, or even win the ballgame."</p><p>Smith looked a little better in his last big-league game, but had another uneven outing in his first go with the Knights over the weekend, giving up three runs on four hits with a walk and five strikeouts in four innings.</p><p>“There’s always a cascade effect when you have starters not going deep into games. It starts affecting bullpens,” Getz said. “He definitely made progress in the most recent outing up here at the major league level, but we know that he can be so much better, and he acknowledges that as well.”</p><h3><b>Pre-game scare</b></h3><p>Attendees at Rate Field were left in a stunned silence before Wednesday’s game when <b>Gerald Chaney</b> collapsed while singing "Lift Every Voice and Sing” on Jackie Robinson Day.</p><p>Chaney, a longtime national anthem performer and beloved ballpark presence, was alert as paramedics treated him on the field. He was taken to a hospital for further evaluation and treatment, according to the Sox.</p><h3><b>Schultz in the rotation</b></h3><p>Manager <b>Will Venable</b> confirmed that rookie lefty <b>Noah Schultz</b> has a regular spot in the Sox’ starting rotation after his <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/04/14/noah-schultz-white-sox-mlb-debut" target="_blank" ><u>shaky MLB debut</u></a> Tuesday night — no surprise, but an assertion that Getz stopped short of making a day earlier.</p><p>Schultz consistently flashed 98 on the radar gun and struck out four while laboring through 4⅓ innings, giving up four runs but rebounding from some first-inning jitters.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-c30000" name="image-c30000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" alt="Noah Schultz walks to the dugout prior to his MLB debut Tuesday." srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/6885599/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6422x3604+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F56%2F02%2F041a2c464b319cf569f7b96372a3%2F2271388905.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/2711568/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6422x3604+0+0/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F56%2F02%2F041a2c464b319cf569f7b96372a3%2F2271388905.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/6885599/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6422x3604+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F56%2F02%2F041a2c464b319cf569f7b96372a3%2F2271388905.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>The White Sox’ Noah Schultz walks to the dugout prior to his MLB debut Tuesday.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Michael Reaves/Getty Images</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>"The sky is the limit for him, man,” said catcher <b>Edgar Quero</b>. “Not many guys like him right now in the big leagues."</p><p>Soft-spoken in the clubhouse but intense on the mound, the 6-10 Schultz credited big sisters <b>Emily Schultz</b> and <b>Ashley Schultz</b> with sharpening his on-field edge in their childhood Wiffle ball games in the backyard while <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/04/13/noah-schultz-white-sox-debut-aurora-oswego-east" target="_blank" ><u>growing up in Aurora</u></a>.</p><p>“They helped get that competitive spirit in me. It started at a young age,” Schultz said. “I can’t thank them enough. They have been with me through it all. It’s so awesome to have their support and have them in the stands.”</p><h3><b>Coming and going</b></h3><p>As part of the Sox’ <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/04/15/sam-antonacci-called-up-white-sox" target="_blank" ><u>latest roster shuffle</u></a> bringing up prospect <b>Sam Antonacci</b>, they also recalled veteran lefty reliever <b>Tyler Gilbert</b>, optioned <b>Brandon Eisert</b> to Charlotte and designated outfielder <b>Dustin Harris</b> for assignment.</p><p>Harris was a hero of Sunday’s win at Kansas City, robbing a potential home run in right field. He went 3-for-12 with a double, four walks and a pair of stolen bases in six games with the Sox.</p><p>Venable said they’ll send him to Charlotte if he clears waivers.</p><p>“It was a really tough decision. … He was really just kind of the odd guy out, really not because of anything he did,” Venable said.</p><div class="PageListL" data-module data-hide-authors="true" data-hide-categories="true" entries.title: White Sox national anthem performer Gerald Chaney back to singing after pre-game collapse
entries.summary: <p>Attendees at Rate Field were left in a stunned silence before Wednesday’s White Sox game when Gerald Chaney collapsed while singing “Lift Every Voice and Sing” on Jackie Robinson Day.</p><p>Chaney, a longtime national anthem performer and beloved ballpark presence, was alert as paramedics treated him on the field. He was taken to a hospital for evaluation and doing well by the end of the game — “even singing,” according to the Sox.</p><p>“The entire White Sox family is sending love to Gerald and his family for a full and speedy recovery,” the team said in a statement.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-600000" name="image-600000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/e88797b/2147483647/strip/true/crop/1800x1010+0+0/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F10%2Fa7%2F3c33490947b3a7b987657b17d485%2Fsox-cst-062412-4.JPG 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/51cfee9/2147483647/strip/true/crop/1800x1010+0+0/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F10%2Fa7%2F3c33490947b3a7b987657b17d485%2Fsox-cst-062412-4.JPG 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/fc0eb07/2147483647/strip/true/crop/1800x1010+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F10%2Fa7%2F3c33490947b3a7b987657b17d485%2Fsox-cst-062412-4.JPG" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="Gerald Chaney, pictured singing the national anthem before a Sox game on June 22, 2012. " srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/fc0eb07/2147483647/strip/true/crop/1800x1010+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F10%2Fa7%2F3c33490947b3a7b987657b17d485%2Fsox-cst-062412-4.JPG 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/7c71a85/2147483647/strip/true/crop/1800x1010+0+0/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F10%2Fa7%2F3c33490947b3a7b987657b17d485%2Fsox-cst-062412-4.JPG 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/fc0eb07/2147483647/strip/true/crop/1800x1010+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F10%2Fa7%2F3c33490947b3a7b987657b17d485%2Fsox-cst-062412-4.JPG" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Gerald Chaney, pictured singing the national anthem before a Sox game on June 22, 2012. </p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Scott Stewart/Sun-Times file</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>The start of the game was delayed 12 minutes as crews tended to Chaney.</p><p>“Scary stuff,” Sox manager Will Venable said after <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/04/15/sam-antonacci-called-up-white-sox" >his squad’s 8-3 loss</a>. “I was really glad to hear that he is doing well, but obviously a scary moment. I think everyone did a great job in responding and did the best to make sure he was alright. So, really good news to hear that he is alright.”</p><p>Sox starter Sean Burke was warming up when the medical emergency unfolded.</p><p>“They said he's doing well at the hospital now, so hopefully he's going to make a full recovery from that,” Burke said. "I was just trying to make sure he was all good, first and foremost.”</p>
entries.author: Mitchell Armentrout

entries.title: White Sox call up Sam Antonacci looking for jolt to stagnant offense
entries.summary: <p><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox" target="_blank" ><u>White Sox</u></a> prospect Sam Antonacci got news of his promotion from Triple-A Charlotte about 20 hours before he took the field for his debut at Rate Field on Wednesday. </p><p>“Tired, but adrenaline high,” the Springfield native said inside his first big-league clubhouse after a whirlwind travel day. “Get a cup of coffee in you and you’re good to go.”</p><p>It worked for him while playing <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/03/19/sam-antonacci-returns-to-spring-training-says-wbc-experience-was-definitely-worth-it" >for Team Italy</a>, whose run to the World Baseball Classic semifinal was famously fueled by a dugout espresso machine also enjoyed by another caffeine-cranked Sox player, Kyle Teel.</p><p>Now the Sox are looking for a shot in the arm offensively from Antonacci, a utility player whose hot bat to start the year made him the club’s second key prospect call-up in as many days, following the debut of <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/04/14/noah-schultz-white-sox-mlb-debut" target="_blank" ><u>prized left-hander Noah Schultz</u></a>.</p><p>“Excited to add his energy and his skillset to the group,” manager Will Venable said. “He's gonna play extremely hard, control the zone, play solid defense and run the bases extremely well.”</p><p>The Sox’ No. 9-rated prospect wasted little time checking some of those boxes with his parents, sister and 10,192 of his newest fans in attendance. Antonacci vacuumed up a grounder to second base for his first big-league defensive assist to close the first inning, and then dropped a single into right field in his first career at-bat to open the Sox’ half of the second on Wednesday against the Rays. </p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="TweetUrl"> <blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">First big league hit for Sam Antonacci! <a href="https://t.co/p2h7zrD4DF">pic.twitter.com/p2h7zrD4DF</a></p>— Chicago White Sox (@whitesox) <a href="https://twitter.com/whitesox/status/2044572659208400997?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">April 16, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script> </div> </div> </div></div><p>Later in his 1-for-3 night, he made a diving stop on a hot grounder up the middle, and drew a ninth-inning walk that started the Sox' only offensive onslaught during an otherwise lifeless 8-3 loss that saw catcher Reese McGuire pitching in garbage time.</p><p>"It was a dream come true, to be honest. Obviously not the end result," Antonacci said. "As much as I like to be even-keeled and keep my head on straight with the job at task, I needed to take a step back and really take it all in, because I’m living out a dream I had when I was a little kid."</p><p>Though the 2024 fifth-round draft pick debuted at second, he got to the Sox three weeks into the season because he was learning left field on the job at Charlotte, fast-tracking his path in an organization lacking outfield depth. Antonacci mostly played second last year across three levels of the farm system. </p><p>Venable said he had him batting fifth and playing second in his debut only because it coincided with a previously scheduled day off for regular Chase Meidroth.</p><p>"It's really quality at-bats, where he's making good swing decisions, really grinding," Venable said of his newest rookie.</p><p>While Schultz joins a starting rotation that has been surprisingly sturdy, a stagnant Sox offense is desperate for a spark from Antonacci. He joined a team sitting dead last in baseball in batting average (.192), on-base percentage (.281), slugging percentage (.305), hits (107) and runs (54).</p><p>Antonacci’s call-up was hastened by his .313/.500/.479 (15-for-48) start with the Knights, with two doubles, two home runs, seven RBI and five stolen bases.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Sam Antonacci of Team Italy makes a play at shortstop during the World Baseball Classic at loanDepot park on March 16, 2026 in Miami, Florida.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Megan Briggs/Getty</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>“You look at his surface-level numbers and underneath the hood, how productive he has been — his offensive process has always been really advanced,” said general manager Chris Getz, who’s not concerned about a position switch dogging Antonacci like it did for past prospects like Andrew Vaughn.</p><p>“He's made some really good plays and there have been some plays that are a little bit foreign to him. He’s getting more comfortable in the outfield,” Getz said.</p><p>Antonacci said he’s had fun with the switch, which has come with surprises.</p><p>“You get heckled a lot. I didn’t realize that. You don’t really get heckled in the infield,” he said, inviting constructive criticism. “If I’m ever seen as taking a play off, I would like the fans to let me know or have someone else let me know. I’ll get that fixed, but I don’t see that happening.”</p><p>Antonacci <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/04/13/noah-schultz-white-sox-debut-aurora-oswego-east" target="_blank" ><u>and Schultz</u></a> are sure to be joined by more of their Triple-A teammates in a pivotal year for Getz’s rebuild, with debuts anticipated for infielder William Bergolla Jr. and pitchers Hagen Smith and Tanner McDougal.</p><p>“It’s awesome to think about,” Antonacci said. “To wear this White Sox jersey, if it’s for a day or if it’s for the next 10 years, I’m going to go out there and give it all I’ve got. That’s kind of the motto here, and the young guys that we’ve got, we’re leaving it all out there each and every night.”</p><div class="PageListL" data-module data-hide-authors="true" entries.title: 2 teen girls arrested, accused of battering Cook County sheriff's deputies
entries.summary: <p>Two 15-year-old girls were arrested after allegedly battering Cook County sheriff's officers responding to reports of gunfire in Woodlawn Wednesday afternoon, according to Chicago police.</p><p>The sheriff's officers were in the 6400 block of South Evans Avenue at 4:41 p.m. when they saw several people fighting. When they tried to intervene, the two teens allegedly battered the officers, police said. The teens were arrested at the scene.</p><p>One officer was taken to St. Bernard Hospital in good condition, police said.</p>
entries.author: Sun-Times Wire

entries.title: Nico Hoerner, Matt Shaw dominate as Cubs grab series win over Phillies
entries.summary: <p>PHILADELPHIA —<b> Matt Shaw</b> and <b>Nico Hoerner</b> formed quite the duo in the Cubs' Wednesday’s 11-2 win.</p><p>Shaw would double, and Hoerner drove him in.</p><p>Hoerner's table-setting ways have been pronounced in the last two games of the Phillies series. His eight RBIs over the past two games were the most he’s recorded over a two-game span. The offense is at its best when it strings together hits and has productive at-bats, particularly from the bottom of the order. On all three of Shaw’s doubles, Hoerner drove him in.</p><p>“I thought it was a big night for Matt,” manager Craig Counsell said. “When your No. 9 hitter gets three doubles, that's a good sign.”</p><p>Hoerner was 3-for-5 with a career-high five RBIs. Shaw was 3-for-4 with two RBIs. The ideal version of the Cubs’ lineup involves production from the entire lineup. With first-round picks at six spots, the group has the pedigree and talent to be a problem on a daily basis.</p><p>If the hitters start producing towards their career norms then the offense should be able to carry the bulk of the responsibility while the pitching staff is depleted.</p><p>“We should go out there and expect to have good at-bats,” Shaw said. Things are starting to fall a little bit, so t's awesome to get some momentum and have the boys run.”</p><p><b>Imanaga dealing</b></p><p>As of late, left-hander<b> Shota Imanaga </b>has resembled the pitcher who took MLB by storm in 2024.</p><p>Wednesday’s start got off to an inauspicious start. Imanaga allowed a leadoff homer to Trea Turner, but that was the extent of the damage he allowed Wednesday. Imanaga regrouped and mowed through the Phillies’ lineup. He allowed one run on three hits with one walk and 11 strikeouts. His four-seamer sat at 92 mph.</p><p>“We talk so much about velocity in general,” Hoerner said, “I think it is different with him throwing 93 instead of 90. Every bit of life on that four-seamer he has is a huge deal.”</p><p>Imanaga is pitching with the confidence he seemingly lost toward the end of last season. His 26 whiffs were tied for the most in one outing by a Cubs pitcher in the pitch-tracking era — <b>Yu Darvish</b> also recorded 26 against the White Sox in 2020.</p><p><b>Injury report</b></p><p>The Cubs’ injury report seemingly grows longer by the day. Cubs manager <b>Craig Counsell </b>announced that right-hander <b>Porter Hodge</b> would miss the rest of the season after having UCL reconstruction surgery.</p><p>• Opening Day starter <b>Matthew Boyd</b> is headed to Ohio and will pitch on Thursday for Triple-A Iowa. That puts him on schedule to return to the Cubs around next Wednesday to finish a seven-game homestand against the Phillies.</p><p>• Left-hander <b>Jordan Wicks</b> (left elbow inflammation) is scheduled to pitch Saturday for Triple-A Iowa. He’s been out since spring training.</p><p>• Right-hander<b> Hunter Harvey </b>(right triceps inflammation)<b> </b>is likely going to take longer than the 15 days on the IL. Counsell that Harvey is in strengthening mode.</p><p>• Right-hander <b>Phil Maton</b> (right knee tendinitis) had an optimistic update. Counsell said he’s recovering each day. Phil didn’t stop throwing after he was placed on the 15-day IL, so now it’s a matter of getting the arm back to game action.<br></p>
entries.author: Kyle Williams

entries.title: Jed Hoyer to 'come up with real options' for Cubs' injured pitching staff
entries.summary: <p>PHILADELPHIA — Injuries have put <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/cubs" >Cubs</a> president of baseball operations Jed Hoyer on the prowl for starting pitching.</p><p>“You just immediately feel sorry for the player, but then you just process like, ‘Who’s up next?’ ” Hoyer said before the game Wednesday against the Phillies. “You have to move on quick mentally. It sounds cold-hearted, but that’s the nature of the job. The games go on, you have to find replacements.”</p><p>Cubs manager Craig Counsell announced that right-handed reliever Porter Hodge suffered a setback recovering from a flexor strain and will miss the rest of the season after undergoing surgery on his ulnar collateral ligament. He began the season on the 15-day injured list and was seen as a player who could help the team.</p><p>Hodge, whom the Cubs thought could rebound from a rough 2025 season (6.27 ERA), now joins promising right-hander Cade Horton as pitchers lost for the season. Hoyer said that Hodge felt some discomfort early in spring training.</p><p>With relievers Ethan Roberts (middle finger laceration), Hunter Harvey (right triceps) and Phil Maton (knee) all sidelined, the Cubs’ bullpen has become increasingly unbalanced with an unusually large number of left-handers. Given the sheer number of injuries, it’s fair to wonder if the Cubs are assessing their own pitching processes.</p><p>“You have to take a look because you wouldn’t be doing your job if you didn’t,” Hoyer said. “A lot of guys go down these days. Guys throw so unbelievably hard, but we’ll do a deep dive and see if there’s a commonality to the injuries.”</p><p>Entering Wednesday, Cubs relievers had the 15th-best ERA in baseball (4.04). Left-hander Riley Martin has helped the Cubs survive this stretch (4„ scoreless innings).</p><p>Hoyer said that the club is looking at the injuries as an opportunity for others to emerge. Hodge wasn’t on the Cubs’ radar early in the 2024 season, but he received a chance to show how he could deliver in the majors and ran with it (1.88 ERA).</p><p>“There’s opportunities being created for guys,” Counsell said. “Through this process, we’re going to find some guys that are going to help us this year, and so that’s how you’ve got to look at it. That’s a great thing. That’s a positive out of all this.”</p><p>The offense’s improvement also should help the pitching staff. The way hitters were aggressive and opportunistic in Tuesday’s 10-4 win over the Phillies is how many envisioned the offense before the season. Right-hander Colin Rea, who pitched six innings and allowed three runs, said the offensive production made it easier for him to pitch.</p> <p>“Somehow, our run-scoring numbers aren’t that bad,” Hoyer said. “But I think [when] we look up and down the lineup, you have so many guys that are so far below career norms, especially in [slugging] — I look at it as a real opportunity. I’m almost excited about it because we’re 17 games in [and] only a couple guys are sort of above their projections.”</p><p>As the offense finds its footing, Hoyer has to survey the market for capable replacements.</p><p>“The schedule’s not gonna stop,” Hoyer said. “We gotta find ways to come up with new guys and come up with real options. Hopefully, some of those guys can step up.”</p><bsp-listcarousel class="PageListE" data-module data-column-count=3 data-hide-authors="true" data-hide-categories="true" data-hide-dates="true" entries.author: Kyle Williams

entries.title: Blackhawks owner Danny Wirtz announces contract extension for GM Kyle Davidson
entries.summary: <p><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/blackhawks" >Blackhawks</a> owner Danny Wirtz announced a multi-year contract extension Wednesday for general manager Kyle Davidson, who's wrapping up his fourth season in the position.</p><p>GM contracts are generally extended consistently until they're fired — it's rare to see one leave an NHL team simply because his contract expired — but it's still notable.</p><p>Wirtz also gave Davidson a firm and extensive vote of confidence on the Chicago Sports Network pregame show.</p><p>"Kyle has done everything we've expected of him," Wirtz said. "When he got the job, his first task was to rebuild our prospect pool. ... He very systematically, very confidently built that to where I believe, right now, we have the best prospect pool in the NHL. Many of those prospects are now actually NHL players, contributing and highly impactful on our team."</p><p>"But at the same time, we didn't hire Kyle to be the prospect GM. He's building a championship-caliber team. It goes to the point we've extended Kyle's contract. We are committing to Kyle to continue the plan he's put in place. We feel confident in that. We believe has the right insight, right team around him and the belief he can continue to build a championship team."</p><p>In a news conference later, Wirtz mentioned the Hawks being more competitive on the ice this season, improving their point total despite losing a plethora of close games, as a sign of improvement.</p><p>Of course, that competitiveness hasn't held true over the past month, during which time the roster has most closely resembled the long-term roster. But Wirtz said he wasn't alarmed by that.</p><p>"[It's] probably a reminder and a recognition of just how hard this league is, especially for players going from leagues where they play considerably less games, less travel," he said.</p><p>"I don’t think that, in any way, shakes my confidence for the team that’s being built... I expect them to continue to grow next season."</p><p>He conceded he would be disappointed next season if the Hawks "stayed flat to this year" and finished in the league's bottom-five again.</p><p>And he clarified Davidson has a green light to make significant trades this summer if the opportunity arises: "There’s nothing holding Kyle back, that’s for sure. It’s not coming from me."</p><p>But he continues to focus most of his attention well into the future.</p><p>"These players, in their own development, have to take another step," Wirtz said. "Even Connor [Bedard] needs to take another step, and he will. He’s that competitive. </p><p>"The collective improvement each of those players makes translates into team competitiveness and ultimately wins. That’s what we’re going for. Our intention is to be competing for and winning Stanley Cups, but we can’t race to that conclusion until we do all those right things."</p><bsp-list-loadmore class="PageListH" data-module entries.title: 2 teens shot, 1 fatally, at Woodlawn bus stop
entries.summary: <p>Two teens were shot, one fatally, at a bus stop in Woodlawn Wednesday afternoon, according to Chicago police.</p><p>The teens, a boy and girl, both 16, were at a bus stop in the 6300 block of South Stony Island Avenue at 3:23 p.m. when a person approached and opened fire, police said. The boy was hit multiple times and taken to University of Chicago Medical Center in critical condition; he later died.</p><p>The girl was shot in her left leg and also taken to University of Chicago Medical Center in fair condition, police said. No one is in custody.</p><p></p><p>Chicago Police Supt. Larry Snelling addressed recent incidents of violence involving young people during a hearing on the federal consent decree Tuesday.</p><p>“We have young kids who are now taking to the streets, not only in Chicago but across the country ... and they’re engaging in activity that has led to violence,” Snelling said.</p><p>Snelling said the police department had seen "more CPS students shot and killed this year than we have in the past 10 years, possibly." According to Chicago Police Department homicide data, 2026 is on track to see the third consecutive year in which youth homicides have fallen by mid-April with 13.</p><p>The city saw 13 youth homicides by this point in the year in 2019 and 2020, though that number nearly doubled in 2023, reaching 24. The tally for the same time period dropped in 2024 and has continued to decline since. Annually, youth homicides have also been declining for several years.</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/education/2021/12/20/22836535/cps-trauma-public-schools-student-murders-killed-pandemic-lavizzo-simeon-crown" >Murders of students amid the pandemic strain schools’ ability to cope, heal</a> </li> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/crime/2026/01/01/chicago-crime-murder-homicide-shooting-president-trump-mayor-johnson" >Chicago records fewest annual killings since 1965</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p></p><p></p><p></p>

entries.title: Horoscope for Thursday, April 16, 2026
entries.summary: <h2>Moon Alert</h2> <p>There are no restrictions to shopping or important decisions today. The moon is in Aries.</p><h2>Aries (March 21-April 19)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-6f0000" name="html-embed-module-6f0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>dynamic</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> This is a powerful day for you. Mercury in your sign is lined up with Neptune, while your ruler fiery Mars gets a boost from Pluto. This means your ability to magnetize things is amazing and unimaginable. You can seduce, persuade and cajole anyone to get what you want. Be responsible.</p><h2>Taurus (April 20-May 20)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> A powerful behind-the-scenes energy is at work today, which you might or might not be able to harness. If you can use it, you will set things in motion to go in the direction you want. You'll be persuasive and irresistible. However, if you can't harness this energy, you might feel out of control.</p><h2>Gemini (May 21-June 20)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Your popularity is strong! Today you can work with others, either in a partnership or in conjunction with a group or an organization, to make changes for the better. You'll be zealous about achieving your goals. Work with others to get what you want!</p><h2>Cancer (June 21-July 22)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; Use it.</p><h2>Sagittarius (Nov. 22-Dec. 21)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-6f0000" name="html-embed-module-6f0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>dynamic</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> This is an amazing day for those in sports, the entertainment world, or the hospitality industry because you have energy to burn! Today you can wrap things up because people will listen to you. You might help a family member. You might improve a family relationship or improve your home. Do it.</p><h2>Aquarius (Jan. 20-Feb. 18)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-6f0000" name="html-embed-module-6f0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>dynamic</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> If you're in sales, marketing, teaching, acting or writing, you're unstoppable today because your powers of persuasion and ability to attract people to you are remarkable. Think carefully about what you want, then state your case. Don't back down. You're in the zone!</p><h2>Pisces (Feb. 19-March 20)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Financial discussions will go well today because you are imaginative and persuasive. You see what you want, and you're not afraid to go after it. More than that, you're not afraid to wish for a miracle. Trust your powers of seduction.</p><h2>If today is your birthday</h2><p>Actress Claire Foy (1984) shares your birthday today. You are caring, intellectual and dedicated. Family and community matter to you. This year is your turn to socialize and enjoy life because your creativity and zest are at a pinnacle. Nurture the happiness and beauty that you have within you. Let your guard down and loosen up a bit.</p>
entries.author: Georgia Nicols

entries.title: Blackhawks end season with feel-good comeback win over Sharks
entries.summary: <p>The first two periods of the <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/blackhawks" target="_blank" >Blackhawks</a>' season finale Wednesday mirrored their last few weeks: Sloppy, slow and worrying.</p><p>Then a lucky goal off Sam Rinzel's stick late in the second period shifted momentum, and it seemed the Hawks suddenly discovered an extra reserve energy tank, racing away for a 5-2 win over the Sharks.</p><p>They dominated the third period, getting two beautiful goals from Louis Crevier, two assists from Connor Bedard (both saucy backhand passes) and a redemption goal by Ryan Greene, who had missed an empty net earlier.</p><p>"We wanted to finish on a good note, even though the last couple weeks have been hard," Crevier said. "It doesn't erase what happened, but we showed some character in the third."</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="TweetUrl"> <blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">Bedard sets up Crevier for the game-tying goal 😮‍💨 <a href="https://t.co/qgpUIGRr6H">pic.twitter.com/qgpUIGRr6H</a></p>— Blackhawks on CHSN (@CHSN_Blackhawks) <a href="https://twitter.com/CHSN_Blackhawks/status/2044606274117988549?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">April 16, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script> </div> </div> </div></div><p>Wednesday's result marked the first time the Sharks, demoralized after getting eliminated from playoff contention Monday, lost in regulation after leading at the second intermission all season. </p><p>It also marked just the Hawks' fourth win in their last 18 home games. They finished the season 29-39-14, earning 72 points — up from 61 last season.</p><p>"We were never checked out or anything like that," Bedard said. "We were trying every game. That’s something we want people to know. Obviously [it] didn’t go our way, but there wasn’t a day where we weren’t trying to do everything we can to put out our best effort.</p><p>"Coming into this last game, we really wanted to...show our fans just how much pride we have in playing in the NHL but also playing in Chicago. I’m happy we were able to do that and have a win to end the year."</p><p>Crevier delivered a heroic performance, not only with the two goals but also two crucial blocks on a third-period penalty kill, as he bounced back from what he called one of his worst performances of the season <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/blackhawks/2026/04/13/blackhawks-sabres-effort-levels-crash-frank-nazar-ryan-greene" target="_blank" >Monday</a> against the Sabres.</p><p>His evolution from seventh defenseman into top-four regular was one of the more satisfying under-the-radar trends of this Hawks season. As coach Jeff Blashill put it, "Mental toughness is a huge part of individual success."</p>
entries.author: Ben Pope

entries.title: After another season of strong Blackhawks attendance, it's time to start rewarding fans with more wins
entries.summary: <p>The <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/blackhawks" target="_blank" >Blackhawks</a>’ business department entered this season confident that attendance would be strong, and it was proved correct.</p><p>The Hawks averaged 18,949 fans per game, which ranked fifth in the NHL — behind only the Canadiens, Panthers, Lightning and Red Wings — despite the team finishing 31st in the standings.</p><p>After trending below last season’s average of 18,585 (Winter Classic excluded) for the first month, they ended up easily topping it. </p><p>That’s because since Nov. 23 — the start of <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/blackhawks/2025/12/01/blackhawks-attendance-ticket-sales-united-center-nhl-sharks-wild" target="_blank" >Thanksgiving week</a> — they averaged 19,440 fans per game, dipping below 18,000 only four times. The United Center was full on a remarkably consistent basis.</p><p>This sets a new high-water mark for the Hawks in the five seasons with full capacity since the pandemic, which ended their 535-game sellout streak. Their 18 sellouts this season mark another post-pandemic high.</p><p>"It has been a few years of not making the playoffs...[but] our fans are still behind us for all 41 home games, and that’s pretty special," Connor Bedard said Wednesday. "You see some teams go through similar things, and they don’t have many people in the crowd. We’re extremely lucky."</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-2d0000"
